summary: Blazing China>Rider, tight Deal, emotional Rain encore
blurb: The show features a scorching China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition with Jerry’s dazzling finger‑picked solo and a punchy Deal that drives the first set. The second set builds through Truckin' into Smokestack Lightning and The Other One before an emotionally resonant Rain encore.
review: On June 19, 1993 at Soldier Field, the Dead delivered a night of high‑energy rock punctuated by some of the era’s most memorable moments. Jerry Garcia lit up the stage with an “amazing” finger‑picked solo on China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider that many fans described as shredding and soaring, while Phil Lesh supplied his trademark “wild riffs,” especially during that same segue. The first set closed strong with a crisp, driving Deal, and the second set rolled forward through a thunderous Truckin' into Smokestack Lightning, leading into an exploratory stretch of The Other One and Standing On The Moon before culminating in a heartfelt Rain encore that left the rain‑soaked crowd emotionally satisfied. Though the best soundboard source suffers from static crackle—most noticeable on Deal and the China>Rider pairing—the performance itself remains solid, with the audience recording capturing the atmospheric rain ambience and the spooky drum‑to‑space vibe during Smokestack Lightning. Overall, the show stands out for its fiery improvisations and a moving finale, making it a worthwhile listen despite some sonic blemishes.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings label the show as mixed while one is positive; however, consistent praise for Jerry’s China>Rider solo, Deal’s energy, and the Rain encore outweighs the static and occasional weak moments, yielding a modest positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source captures the full mix and most of the performance despite static, providing the clearest representation of the show.

key_highlights: Jerry’s finger‑picked solo on China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider is a standout moment.
Deal delivers high‑octane energy that drives the first set forward.
The second‑set flow through Truckin' → Smokestack Lightning → The Other One showcases deep improvisational chemistry.
Rain encore provides an emotionally resonant close to a rain‑soaked night.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia shines with an amazing finger‑picked solo on China>Rider, shreds throughout the set and delivers a heartfelt vocal harmony on the Rain encore.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh supplies wild, driving riffs especially during the China>Rider segue and maintains solid, propulsive bass work all night.
band_performance.Mickey: Both drummers create a spacious, atmospheric “drumz>space” vibe during Smokestack Lightning, adding depth to the jam.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey, provides rhythmic power and the spooky drum‑to‑space feel that underpins the second‑set explorations.
